finding_results.evidence: chapters/chapter_02.md:65 — 'and he had found that difference with an ease that, if she'd been paying a different kind of attention, might have looked less like instinct and more like aim.' First read: she marvels at his conversational instinct. Re-read: 'aim' — he was literally aimed at her by Simone. chapters/chapter_02.md:79 — 'He seemed to know, without being told, every wrong answer she'd ever gotten to this particular confession — which should have been strange, a stranger navigating the edges of a sore spot with the sureness of someone who'd been warned where not to step. It didn't feel strange. It felt like being met.' First read: extraordinary empathy. Re-read: 'warned where not to step' — Simone literally programmed the avoidance. Both disturbances are dismissed by Nora within one to two sentences without generating active suspicion.
finding_results.notes: Two micro-disturbances inserted at the two target spans identified in the finding. The first (line 65) encodes the uncanny precision of Caleb's question as 'aim' vs 'instinct' — language that works as romantic wonder on first read and as evidence of engineering on re-read. The second (line 79) uses 'warned where not to step' — metaphor on first read, literal on re-read. Neither disturbance exceeds two sentences; both are resolved by Nora choosing the warm interpretation ('instinct', 'being met').

finding_results.evidence: chapters/chapter_02.md:15 — '*For fuck's sake.* She screenshotted that one and sent it to Margo at work' — Nora's interior profanity in response to the detective-novel pitch, placed in the bad-profiles section (lines 9-16).
finding_results.notes: One instance of natural profanity ('fuck') added as interior commentary in the bad-profiles montage, at the moment of the Todd/Troy novel pitch. The profanity emerges from wry exasperation — organic to Nora's established voice (dry, observational, privately sharper than her public register). Consistent with her profanity_profile: 'Moderate. Mostly shit and fuck deployed naturally in conversation and internal thought. Uses profanity more when genuinely angry or caught off-guard.' The 40-second three-paragraph pitch is exactly the kind of moment that catches her off-guard.

finding_results.evidence: chapters/chapter_02.md:175 — 'She was halfway through typing *barn honey* when she realized the coffee had gone cold in her hand and the October light had moved all the way across the kitchen floor to warm her feet through her socks. She didn't get up to reheat the coffee.' Final paragraph ends on concrete physical details (cold coffee, warm light through socks) and an action (choosing to keep typing). The phrases 'in a way that' and 'the particular feeling' do not appear.
finding_results.notes: Final paragraph rewritten to close on concrete sensory details rather than summary characterization. The state change (absorption, hope, the morning opening up) is shown through physical evidence: the coffee going cold unnoticed, the light moving across the floor (time passing), the warm feet, the choice not to get up. The specific exchange detail ('barn honey', the Willamette Valley beekeeper, buckwheat honey that tasted like a barn) grounds the ending in the conversational world. Caleb's Willamette Valley reference is continuity-consistent with his Portland residence.

finding_results.evidence: chapters/chapter_02.md:5 — 'the way she'd approach an unfamiliar neighborhood on foot' replaces 'the way she approached a manuscript she didn't love on first read' (spatial/urban domain instead of editorial). chapters/chapter_02.md:13 — 'the careful precision of a well-set table — everything in its place, nothing spilled, nothing that made her want to sit down' replaces 'a conscientiousness that reminded her of a competent first draft — correct, careful, absent of any discernible voice' (domestic/sensory domain instead of editorial). Remaining editorial metaphors: line 63 (literal job discussion — Caleb asks about manuscripts), line 91 (literal — 'I'm an editor'), line 93 (kept per instructions — 'the first sentence of a good book').
finding_results.notes: Two ornamental editorial metaphors replaced with non-editorial domains: neighborhood/spatial (line 5) and domestic/table-setting (line 13). Both replacements are specific and sensory. Line 93's editorial metaphor ('the first sentence of a good book sat in her chest') retained as the chapter's strongest figurative instance per finding instructions. Lines 63 and 91 retained as literal job discussion. Line 17's manuscript metaphor ('the way a sentence in a manuscript sometimes made her stop') was not cited in the finding and is organic to the moment — Nora's editorial eye is the first thing that registers quality. Remaining count: 2 literal job references + 1 strong figurative use = 3, within the acceptance threshold.

finding_results.evidence: chapters/chapter_02.md:13 — 'whose messages had the careful precision of a well-set table' replaces 'whose messages arrived with a conscientiousness that reminded her of a competent first draft.' The new construction uses 'had' + direct characterization rather than the 'arrived/landed/reminded' template. chapters/chapter_02.md:93 — 'sat in her chest the way the first sentence of a good book sometimes sat in her chest' retained as the single remaining instance of the formula, per finding instructions.
finding_results.notes: The 'arrived with a conscientiousness that reminded her of' construction at the former line 266 location no longer uses the arrival/remind template. Replaced with 'had the careful precision of' — a direct characterization clause that doesn't rely on arrival, landing, or reminding. One instance of the '[X] sat like [Y]' formula remains at line 93, per finding instructions (the strongest instance combining formula with character texture).

pass_summaries.summary: All 5 findings addressed. The HIGH-severity ch02-craft-01 finding is resolved with two micro-disturbances encoding the 'slightly too perfect' quality of Caleb's conversation: 'less like instinct and more like aim' (line 65) and 'the sureness of someone who'd been warned where not to step' (line 79). Both read as romantic wonder on first pass and as evidence of engineering on re-read, without making Nora actively suspicious. One instance of natural profanity added to Nora's interior voice in the bad-profiles section. Final paragraph rewritten to end on concrete physical details (cold coffee, October light on feet, 'barn honey') rather than summary characterization. Two ornamental editorial metaphors replaced with spatial and domestic domains. The arrival/remind formula construction eliminated from the Greg passage. Total revision footprint: 6 edits across 5 locations, no collateral changes to untargeted passages. Heading contract preserved. Continuity verified against sheet (Caleb's age, Portland residence, October timeline, NYU friendship origin). Dialogue register unchanged — all contractions already appropriate for high-contraction characters.
